title: Print progress of resource creation in podman kube play
body:

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *vyasgun* **Once this PR has been reviewed and has the lgtm label**, please assign n1hility for approval. For more information see [the Kubernetes Code Review Process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process). The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
What problem is this trying to solve? Changing the output format will very likely break people who current parse the output. I know a lot of our tests do.
@Luap99 This PR is for this issue: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/15902 . Can you suggest an alternative to track the progress?

If this is needed for podman-desktop/remote than printing is definitely the wrong choice as this will only ever work locally.

What we would need is to create a new `io.Writer` interface variable which is passed down the play kube stack were we can write the progress to. The caller can then decide what to pass down as `io.Writer` would be, for local podman we could just pass down `os.Stderr` and for the remote API we can pass down the client socket so the output get actually streamed over the socket.

But then the question is how the progress format should look like? This definitely needs some for of consensus/design doc before we try to implement it. I think not breaking the existing output format is required. For local just streaming to stderr might work but we definitely cannot just start writing different output via API as this must be stable and backwards compatible. We can however add a new option to the endpoint so callers can opt into that output.
@Luap99 Thanks for the suggestions. Should I close this PR till the format is decided?

For now yes lets close the PR as this is the wrong approach.

I don't know what the usual podman-desktop process is, we use some google doc as design docs. In any case someone needs to write down what info must be printed and in what format it should be. How we can keep backwards compat, etc... Only then we can talk about implementation.
I am not the person to do that as I have no idea about use case or requirements nor know anything about the kube code at all.
Thanks, I will implement it in another PR with the design from the desktop team. Closing this for now.
